req.title: Transaction dispute
req.statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a strict 60-day window for filing transaction disputes. Three partitions exist: (1) valid disputes where transaction.age < 60 days (EP positive), (2) invalid disputes where transaction.age > 60 days (NEG/EP negative), and (3) the exact boundary at day 60 (BVA). A State Transition test covers the lifecycle from 'dispute submitted' to 'provisional credit applied'. A Decision Table test covers the combination of eligibility factors. BVA dictates testing at day 59 (last valid day), day 60 (exact boundary, expected rejection per strict < 60 rule), and day 61 (clearly invalid). The boundary case tests day 59 as the last accepted value and day 60 as the first rejected value, which is the most critical BVA pair.

tests.name: test_dispute_filed_within_valid_window_creates_dispute_and_provisional_credit
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
A posted transaction exists with age = 30 days (well within the 60-day window)
The transaction has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes with valid transaction_id and dispute reason
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-009: provisional credit applied on valid dispute
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_dispute_filed_within_valid_window_creates_dispute_and_provisional_credit(
    auth_headers, base_url, create_posted_transaction
):
    txn = create_posted_transaction(age_days=30)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "unauthorized_charge",
        "description": "Did not recognize this transaction"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body, "Response missing dispute_id"
    assert body.get("status") == "open", f"Unexpected status: {body.get('status')}"
    credit = body.get("provisional_credit", {})
    assert credit.get("applied") is True, "Provisional credit not applied"

tests.name: test_dispute_filed_beyond_60_days_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
A posted transaction exists with age = 61 days (beyond the 60-day limit)
The transaction has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id referencing a 61-day-old transaction
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating dispute window expired
trace: R-FIN-009: disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_dispute_filed_beyond_60_days_returns_422(
    auth_headers, base_url, create_posted_transaction
):
    txn = create_posted_transaction(age_days=61)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "unauthorized_charge",
        "description": "Late dispute attempt"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/disputes",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error = body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))
    assert "window" in str(error).lower() or "expired" in str(error).lower() or "60" in str(error), (
        f"Error message does not reference dispute window: {error}"
    )

tests.name: test_dispute_at_day_59_accepted_and_day_60_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Two posted transactions exist: one with age = 59 days, one with age = 60 days
Neither transaction has been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes for the 59-day-old transaction; capture response
POST /disputes for the 60-day-old transaction; capture response
expected: 59-day transaction: HTTP 201, dispute created, provisional credit applied
60-day transaction: HTTP 422, dispute rejected (age is NOT strictly less than 60)
trace: R-FIN-009: precondition transaction.age < 60d means day 59 is last valid day
R-FIN-009: day 60 is the first rejected value per BVA on the strict inequality

tests.name: test_dispute_lifecycle_transitions_from_open_to_provisional_credit_applied
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
A posted transaction exists with age = 10 days
The transaction has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes to create a new dispute; verify initial state = 'open'
GET /disputes/{dispute_id} to confirm transition to state with provisional credit
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 and initial status = 'open'
GET returns dispute with provisional_credit.applied = true
Dispute status reflects a valid post-creation state (open or under_review)
trace: R-FIN-009: provisional credit applied (state transition after creation)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_dispute_lifecycle_transitions_from_open_to_provisional_credit_applied(
    auth_headers, base_url, create_posted_transaction
):
    txn = create_posted_transaction(age_days=10)
    payload = {"transaction_id": txn["id"], "reason": "merchandise_not_received"}
    create_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/disputes", json=payload, headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    dispute_id = create_resp.json()["dispute_id"]
    assert create_resp.json().get("status") == "open"

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/disputes/{dispute_id}", headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") in ("open", "under_review"), (
        f"Unexpected state: {body.get('status')}"
    )
    assert body.get("provisional_credit", {}).get("applied") is True

tests.name: test_dispute_decision_table_reason_codes_within_valid_window
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Three posted transactions exist, each with age = 5 days
Each transaction represents a distinct dispute reason category
expected: All three responses contain dispute_id and provisional_credit.applied = true
Each dispute has a distinct dispute_id
trace: R-FIN-009: all valid reason codes within window produce dispute + provisional credit
R-FIN-009: DT covers multiple input conditions (reason) against eligibility
